[[File:Astasahasrika Prajnaparamita Dharmacakra Discourse.jpeg |thumb|right|upright=1.23|The Buddha teaching the Four Noble Truths. [[Sanskrit]] manuscript. [[Nalanda|Nālandā]], [[Bihar]], [[India]].]]
Three provocations

* a realize your agency is threatened by [[shuttering]]
* l shuttering is caused by your mental machinery going against you
* o you can escape shuttering by the [[ninefold vaccine]] using your own mental machinery{{Efn|merger of 3 and 4}}

==Four ennobling provocations ==
Formulation 1<ref name=":0">https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=eJzYzZUoJ3c&list=PLM3v2nl0P_5KTXEXVB1evg8c8fhum5Pdm&index=13</ref>

* Realize that all your life is threatened with a lack of agency (dukkha/spiralling/self destruction)
*Spiralling is caused by reciprocal narrowing (suffering is caused by desire) 
*You can use the same machinery to anagogically ascend
*You can use a counteractive dynamical system against it ([[Counter-Spiralling]]/[[Firmlaw]])
Formulation 2<ref name=":1">[[Reacting to the Meaning Crisis]] episode 36 & 37</ref>

# Realize all your life is threatened by a loss of agency
# Loss of agency is caused by the [[Seven Plagues|Nine plagues]]
# You can use the same machinery to overcome the Nine  plagues
# You can use the [[Ninefold Vaccine]] to overcome the Nine Plagues

== Traditional formulation ==
This is the traditional formulation which is problematic for several reasons<ref name=":0" /><ref name=":1" />

* ''[https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Dukkha dukkha]'' (suffering, incapable of satisfying, painful) is an innate characteristic of existence in the realm of ''[https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Samsara samsara]'';<ref name="EB-4NTa" group="web">[https://www.britannica.com/topic/Four-Noble-Truths Four Noble Truths: BUDDHIST PHILOSOPHY], Encyclopaedia Britannica, Quote: "The first truth, suffering (Pali: dukkha; Sanskrit: duhkha), is characteristic of existence in the realm of rebirth, called samsara (literally “wandering”)."</ref><ref>{{harvnb|Anderson|2004|pp=295–297}}. Quote: "This, bhikkhus, is the noble truth that is suffering. Birth is suffering; old age is suffering; illness is suffering; death is suffering; sorrow and grief, physical and mental suffering, and disturbance are suffering. [...]  In short, all life is suffering, according to the Buddha’s first sermon."</ref>{{sfn|Keown|2013|pp=50–52}}
* ''samudaya'' (origin, arising, combination; 'cause'): together with ''dukkha'' arises ''[https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ta%E1%B9%87h%C4%81 taṇhā]'' ("craving, desire or attachment, lit. "thirst").<ref name="EB-4NTb" group="web">[https://www.britannica.com/topic/Four-Noble-Truths Four Noble Truths: BUDDHIST PHILOSOPHY], Encyclopaedia Britannica, Quote: "The second truth is the origin (Pali and Sanskrit: samudaya) or cause of suffering, which the Buddha associated with craving or attachment in his first sermon."</ref><ref>{{harvnb|Anderson|2004|pp=295–297}}. Quote: "The second truth is samudaya (arising or origin). To end suffering, the four noble truths tell us, one needs to know how and why suffering arises. The second noble truth explains that suffering arises because of craving, desire, and attachment."</ref>{{sfn|Keown|2013|pp=53–55}} While ''tanha'' is traditionally interpreted in western languages as the 'cause' of ''dukkha'', ''tanha'' can also be seen as the factor tying us to ''dukkha'', or as a response to ''dukkha'', trying to escape it;{{sfn|Brazier|2001}}{{sfn|Batchelor|2012|pp=95–97}}
* ''[https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Nirodha nirodha]'' (cessation, ending, confinement): ''dukkha'' can be ended or contained by the renouncement or letting go of this ''taṇhā'';{{sfn|Buswell|Lopez|2014|page="nirodha"}}{{sfn|Anderson|2001|p=96}}<ref>{{harvnb|Anderson|2004|pp=295–297}}, Quote: "The third truth follows from the second: If the cause of suffering is desire and attachment to various things, then the way to end suffering is to eliminate craving, desire, and attachment. The third truth is called nirodha, which means “ending” or “cessation.” To stop suffering, one must stop desiring."</ref>{{sfn|Keown|2013|pp=56–58}}  the confinement of ''tanha'' releases the excessive bind of ''dukkha'';{{sfn|Brazier|2001}}{{sfn|Batchelor|2012|pp=95–97}}
* ''[https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Buddhist_paths_to_awakening magga]'' (path, [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Noble_Eightfold_Path Noble Eightfold Path]) is the path leading to the confinement of ''tanha'' and ''dukkha''.<ref>{{harvnb|Anderson|2004|pp=295–297}}, Quote: "This, bhikkhus, is the noble truth that is the way leading to the ending of suffering. This is the eightfold path of the noble ones: right view, right intention, right speech, right action, right livelihood, right effort, right mindfulness, and right concentration.[..] The Buddha taught the fourth truth, maarga (Pali, magga), the path that has eight parts, as the means to end suffering."</ref>{{sfn|Keown|2013|pp=58–60}}{{sfn|Norman|2003|pp=219, 222}}

== References ==
[[Category:John Vervaeke]]
[[Category:Git synced pages]]
[[sn:Three ennobling provocations]][[Category:Buddhism]]
=== Footnotes ===
<references group="web"/>
=====Buddhist teachers=====
{{div col|colwidth=35em}}
<!-- A -->
* {{Citation| last =Ajahn Sumedho | year =2002 | title =The Four Noble Truths | publisher =Amaravati Publications }}
* {{Citation| author =Ajahn Sucitto | year =2010 | title =Turning the Wheel of Truth: Commentary on the Buddha's First Teaching | publisher =Shambhala }}
<!-- B -->
* {{Citation | last =Batchelor | first =Stephen | year =2012 | title =A Secular Buddhism | journal =Journal of Global Buddhism  |volume=13 |pages=87–107}}
* {{Citation| last = Bhikkhu Bodhi | year =1995a | chapter =Preface | title =The Middle Length Discourses of the Buddha. A New Translation of the Majjhima Nikaya | publisher =Wisdom Publications}}
* {{Citation| last = Bhikkhu Bodhi | year =2011 | title =The Noble Eightfold Path: Way to the End of Suffering |edition= Kindle  | publisher =Independent Publishers Group}}
* {{Citation| last =Brazier | first =David | year =2001 | title =The Feeling Buddha | publisher =Robinson Publishing}}
<!-- C -->
* {{Citation| last =Chogyam Trungpa | year =2009 | title =The Truth of Suffering and the Path of Liberation |editor-last=Leif|editor-first=Judy | publisher =Shambhala }}
<!-- D -->
* {{Citation| last =Dalai Lama| year =1992| title =The Meaning of Life: Buddhist Perspectives on Cause and Effect|others=Translated and edited by Jeffrey Hopkins| publisher =Wisdom}}
* {{Citation| last =Dalai Lama| year =1998| title =The Four Noble Truths| publisher =Thorsons}}
* {{Citation| last =Dhamma | first = Ven. Dr. Rewata | year =1997 | title =The First Discourse of the Buddha | publisher =Wisdom|isbn=0-86171-104-1}}
* {{Citation| last =Duff | first =Tony | year =2008 | title =Contemplation by way of the Twelve Interdependent Arisings| publisher =Padma Karpo Translation Committee| access-date =2008-08-19 | url =http://www.tibet.dk/pktc/gelugpa.htm |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20080123171458/http://www.tibet.dk/pktc/gelugpa.htm |archive-date=2008-01-23}}
<!-- E -->
* {{Citation | last =Epstein | first =Mark | year =2004 | title =Thoughts Without A Thinker: Psychotherapy from a Buddhist Perspective |edition= Kindle| publisher = Basic Books}}
<!-- G -->
* {{Citation| last =Geshe Tashi Tsering | year =2005 | title =The Four Noble Truths: The Foundation of Buddhist Thought, Volume I |edition= Kindle | publisher =Wisdom}}
* {{Citation| last =Geshe Tashi Tsering | year =2006 | title =Buddhist Psychology: The Foundation of Buddhist Thought, Volume III |edition= Kindle | publisher =Perseus Books Group}}
* {{Citation | last =Goenka | first =S.N. | year =2000 | title =The Discourse Summaries | publisher =Pariyatti}}
* {{Citation| last =Goldstein | first = Joseph | year =2002 | title =One Dharma: The Emerging Western Buddhism | publisher =HarperCollins}}
* {{Citation| last =Goldstein | first = Joseph | year =2013 | title =Mindfulness: A Practical Guide to Awakening |edition= Kindle| publisher =Sounds True}}
<!-- H -->
* {{Citation | last =Huxter | first =Malcolm | year =2016| title =Healing the Heart and Mind with Mindfulness: Ancient Path, Present Moment | publisher =Routledge | isbn =978-1-317-50540-2 | url=https://books.google.com/books?id=n2qFCwAAQBAJ}}
<!-- K -->
* {{Citation| last =Khunu Rinpoche | year=2012| title= Vast as the Heavens, Deep as the Sea: Verses in Praise of Bodhicitta  | others=Translated by Thubten Thardo (Gareth Sparham)| publisher=Wisdom |edition= Kindle }}
* {{Citation | last =Khantipalo | first =Bhikkhu Phra | year =2003 | title =Calm and Insight: A Buddhist Manual for Meditators | publisher =Routledge}}
<!-- L -->
* {{Citation| last =Lama Surya Das | year =1997 | title =Awakening the Buddha Within |edition= Kindle | publisher =Broadway Books}}
<!-- M -->
* {{Citation| last =Mingyur Rinpoche| year =2007 | title =The Joy of Living: Unlocking the Secret and Science of Happiness|edition= Kindle| publisher = Harmony}}
<!-- P -->
* {{Citation| last =Pema Chodron | year =2010 | title =Comfortable with Uncertainty: 108 Teachings on Cultivating Fearlessness and Compassion|publisher = Shambhala}}
<!-- R -->
* {{Citation | last =Rahula | first =Walpola | year =2007 | title =What the Buddha Taught | publisher =Grove Press}}
* {{Citation |last=Rahula |first=Walpola |year=2007a |title=What the Buddha Taught |edition=Kindle}}
* {{Citation| last= Ringu Tulku | year =2005 | title =Daring Steps Toward Fearlessness: The Three Vehicles of Tibetan Buddhism| publisher = Snow Lion}}
<!-- T -->
* {{Citation | last= Thich Nhat Hanh | year =1991 | title =Old Path White Clouds| publisher = Parallax Press}}
* {{Citation | last= Thich Nhat Hanh | year =1999 | title =The Heart of the Buddha's Teaching | publisher = Three River Press}}
* {{Citation | last= Traleg Kyabgon| year =2001 | title =The Essence of Buddhism | publisher = Shambhala}}
* {{Citation | last =Tsenshap Serkong Rinpoche | year =1996 | title =Een Lamp voor het Pad naar Verlichting | isbn= 978-90718-86-089 | publisher =Uitgeverij Maitreya}}
{{div col end}}

=====Scholarly sources=====
{{div col|colwidth=35em}}
<!-- A -->
* {{Citation | last =Analayo| year =2013|title =The Chinese Parallels to the Dhammacakkappavattana-sutta (2) | journal =Journal of the Oxford Centre for Buddhist Studies  |volume=5 |pages=9–41 | url =http://jocbs.org/index.php/jocbs/article/viewFile/53/85}}
* {{Citation | last =Anderson | first =Carol | year =1999 | title =Pain and Its Ending: The Four Noble Truths in the Theravada Buddhist Canon | publisher =Routledge}}
* {{Citation | last =Anderson | first =Carol | year =2001 | title =Pain and Its Ending: The Four Noble Truths in the Theravada Buddhist Canon | publisher =Motilall Banarsidas}}
* {{Citation | last =Anderson | first =Carol | year =2003 | chapter =Four Noble Truths | editor-last =Buswell | editor-first =Robert E. | title =Encyclopedia of Buddhism | publisher =Macmillan Reference Books | isbn =978-0-02-865718-9}}
* {{cite book |last=Anderson |first=Carol |year=2004 |title=Encyclopedia of Buddhism |editor=Robert E. Buswell |publisher=MacMillan Reference, Thomson Gale |isbn=0-02-865718-7}}
* {{Citation | last =Anderson | first =Carol | year =2013 | title =Pain and Its Ending: The Four Noble Truths in the Theravada Buddhist Canon | publisher =Routledge}}
<!-- B -->
* {{Citation| last =Barber | first =Anthony W. | year =2008 | title =Buddhism in the Krishna River Valley}}
* {{Citation| last = Bhikkhu Bodhi | year =2016 | chapter =The Transformations of Mindfulness | editor-last1 =Purser | editor-first1 =Ronald E. | editor-last2 =Forbes | editor-first2 =David | editor-last3 =Burke | editor-first3 =Adam | title =Handbook of Mindfulness: Culture, Context, and Social Engagement | publisher =Springer}}
* {{Citation | last =Bronkhorst | first =Johannes | year =1993 | title =The Two Traditions of Meditation in Ancient India | publisher =Motilal Banarsidass Publ.}}
* {{Citation | last =Bucknell | first =Rod | year =1984 | title =The Buddhist to Liberation: An Analysis of the Listing of Stages | journal =The Journal of the International Association of Buddhist Studies  |volume=7 |issue=2 | url =https://journals.ub.uni-heidelberg.de/index.php/jiabs/article/viewFile/8631/2538}}
* {{Citation | editor-last1 =Buswell | editor-first1 =Robert E. Jr. | editor-last2 =Gimello | editor-first2 =Robert M. | year =1994 | title =Paths to Liberation. The Marga and its Transformations in Buddhist Thought | place =Delhi | publisher =Motilal Banarsidass Publishers}}
* {{Citation | last1 =Buswell | first1 =Robert E. Jr. | last2 =Lopez | first2 =Donald Jr. | year =2003 | title =The Princeton Dictionary of Buddhism | publisher =Princeton University Press}}
* {{Citation | last1 =Buswell | last2 =Lopez | year =2014 | title =Princeton Dictionary of Buddhism | publisher =Princeton University Press}}
<!-- C -->
* {{Citation | last =Carter | first =John Ross | year =1987 | chapter =Four Noble Truths | editor-last =Jones | editor-first =Lindsay | title =MacMillan Encyclopedia of Religions | publisher =MacMillan}}
* {{Citation | last =Chitkara | first =M. G. | year =1998 | title =Buddhism, Reincarnation, and Dalai Lamas of Tibet | publisher =APH Publishing}}
* {{Citation | last =Choong | first =Mun-keat | year =2000 | title =The Fundamental Teachings of Early Buddhism: A Comparative Study Based on the Sutranga Portion of the Pali Samyutta-Nikaya and the Chinese Samyuktagama | publisher =Otto Harrassowitz Verlag}}
* {{Citation | last =Cohen | first =Robert S. | year =2006 | title =Beyond Enlightenment: Buddhism, Religion, Modernity | publisher =Routledge}}
* {{Citation | last =Coleman | first =James William | year =2002 | title =The New Buddhism. The Western Transformation of an Ancient Tradition | publisher =Oxford University Press}}
* {{Citation | last =Cousins | first =L.S. | year =2001 |title =Review of ''Pain and its Ending: The Four Noble Truths in the Theravada Buddhist Canon'' | journal =Journal of Buddhist Ethics  |volume=8 |pages=36–41 | url=http://blogs.dickinson.edu/buddhistethics/files/2010/04/cousins0111.pdf}}
* {{Citation | last =Crosby | first =Kate | year =2013 | title =Theravada Buddhism: Continuity, Diversity, and Identity | publisher =John Wiley & Sons}}
<!-- D -->
* {{Citation |last = Davidson |first = Ronald M. |year = 2003 |title = Indian Esoteric Buddhism|publisher=Columbia University Press |isbn = 0-231-12618-2}}
<!-- E -->
* {{Citation | last =Egge | first =James | year =2013 | title =Religious Giving and the Invention of Karma in Theravada Buddhism | publisher =Routledge}}
* {{Citation | last =Eliot | first =Charles | year =2014 | title =Japanese Buddhism | publisher =Routledge | isbn =978-1-317-79274-1 | url =https://books.google.com/books?id=EIzsAgAAQBAJ}}
* {{Citation | last =Emmanuel | first =Steven M. | year =2015 | title =A Companion to Buddhist Philosophy | publisher =John Wiley & Sons}}
<!-- F -->
* {{Citation | last=Flanagan | first =Owen | year =2011 | title =The Bodhisattva's Brain: Buddhism Naturalized | publisher =MIT Press | isbn =978-0-262-29723-3}}
* {{Citation | last =Flanagan | first =Owen | year =2014 | title =Science for Monks: Buddhism and Science: A BIT of The Really Hard Problem | publisher =MIT Press}}
* {{Citation | last=Fowler | first =Merv | year =1999 | title =Buddhism: Beliefs and Practices | publisher =Sussex Academic Press | isbn =978-1-898723-66-0}}
* {{Citation | last =Fronsdal | first =Gil | year =1998 | chapter =Insight Meditation in the United States: Life, Liberty, and the Pursuit of Happiness | editor-last1 =Prebish | editor-first1 =Charles S. | editor-last2 =Tanaka | editor-first2 =Kenneth K. | title =The Faces of Buddhism in America | publisher =University of California Press | chapter-url =http://www.insightmeditationcenter.org/books-articles/articles/insight-meditation-in-the-united-states-life-liberty-and-the-pursuit-of-happiness/}}
* {{Citation| last =Fronsdal | first =Gil| year =2001 | title =The Issue at Hand | edition= Kindle | publisher = self-published}}
<!-- G -->
* {{Citation | last1 =Geisler | first1 =Norman | last2 =Amano | first2 =J. Yutaka | year =2004 | title =The Reincarnation Sensation | publisher =Wipf and Stock Publishers}}
* {{Citation | last =Gethin | first = Rupert | year =1998 | title =Foundations of Buddhism | publisher =Oxford University Press}}
* {{Citation | last =Gethin | first =R.M. | year =2003 | title =The Buddhist Path to Awakening | publisher =OneWorld Publications}}
* {{Citation | last1 =Gombrich | first1 =Richard | last2 =Obeyesekere | first2 =Ganan | year =1988 | title =Buddhism Transformed: Religious Change in Sri Lanka | publisher =Motilall Banarsidass}}
* {{Citation | last =Gombrich | first =Richard F. | title =How Buddhism Began: The Conditioned Genesis of the Early Teachings | year =1997 | publisher =Routledge | isbn =978-1-134-19639-5 }}
* {{Citation | last =Gombrich | first =Richard | year =2009 | title =What the Buddha Thought | publisher =Equinox}}
* {{Citation | last=Gowans | first =Christopher | year =2004 | title =Philosophy of the Buddha: An Introduction | publisher =Routledge | isbn =978-1-134-46973-4}}
* {{Citation | last=Gowans | first =Christopher W. | year =2014 | title =Buddhist Moral Philosophy: An Introduction | publisher =Routledge | isbn =978-1-317-65935-8}}
<!-- H -->
* {{Citation | last =Harris | first =Elizabeth | year =2006 | title =Theravada Buddhism and the British Encounter: Religious, Missionary and Colonial Experience in Nineteenth Century Sri Lanka | publisher =Routledge}}
* {{Citation | last =Harvey | first =Graham | year =2016 | title =Religions in Focus: New Approaches to Tradition and Contemporary Practices | publisher =Routledge}}
* {{Citation | last =Harvey | first =Peter | year =2013 |title =An Introduction to Buddhism | publisher =Cambridge University Press}}
* {{Citation | last =Harvey | first =Peter | editor-last =Emmanuel | editor-first=Steven M. | year =2015 | title =A Companion to Buddhist Philosophy | publisher =John Wiley & Sons | isbn =978-1-119-14466-3 | url =https://books.google.com/books?id=P_lmCgAAQBAJ}}
* {{Citation | last =Hayes | first =Richard P. | year =2013  |chapter =The Internet as Window onto American Buddhism | editor-last1 =Queen | editor-first =Christopher | editor-last2 =Williams | editor-first2 =Duncan Ryuken | title =American Buddhism: Methods and Findings in Recent Scholarship | publisher =Routledge}}
* {{Citation | last =Hick | first =John | year =1994 | title =Death and Eternal Life | publisher =Westminster John Knox Press}}
* {{Citation | last =Hirakawa | first =Akira | year =1990 | title =A History of Indian Buddhism. From Sakyamuni to Early Mahayana | publisher =University of Hawai'i Press| hdl =10125/23030 }}
<!-- J -->
* {{Citation | last =Jayatilleke | first =K.N. | year =2009 | title =Facets of Buddhist Thought: Collected Essays | publisher =Buddhist Publication Society}}
<!-- K -->
* {{Citation| last =Kalupahana | first =David J. | year =1992 | title =A history of Buddhist philosophy | place =Delhi | publisher =Motilal Banarsidass Publishers Private Limited}}
* {{Citation | last =Karunyakara | first =Lella | year =2002 | title =Modernisation of Buddhism: Contributions of Ambedkar and Dalai Lama XIV | publisher =Gyan Books}}
* {{Citation| last =Keown | first =Damien | year =2000 | title= Buddhism: A Very Short Introduction |edition= Kindle | publisher=Oxford University Press}}
* {{Citation | last =Keown | first =Damien | year =2009 | title= Buddhism | publisher =Sterling Publishing | isbn =978-1-4027-6883-5}}
* {{Citation |last=Keown |first=Damien |year=2013 |title=Buddhism: A Very Short Introduction |publisher=Oxford University Press |isbn=978-0-19-966383-5 |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=_QXX0Uq29aoC}}
* {{Citation | last =Kingsland | first =James | year =2016 | title =Siddhartha's Brain: Unlocking the Ancient Science of Enlightenment | publisher =HarperCollins}}
* {{Citation | last =Konik | first =Adrian | year =2009 | title =Buddhism and Transgression: The Appropriation of Buddhism in the Contemporary West | publisher =BRIIL}}
<!-- L -->
* {{Citation | last =Lamb | first =Christopher | year =2001 | chapter =Cosmology, myth and symbolism | editor-last =Harvey | editor-first =Peter | title =Buddhism | publisher =Bloomsbury Publishing}}
* {{Citation| last =Leifer | first =Ron| year =1997 | title =The Happiness Project | publisher =Snow Lion }}
* {{Citation |last = Lopez |first = Donald S |year = 1995 |title = Buddhism in Practice |publisher = Princeton University Press |isbn = 0-691-04442-2 |url = https://archive.org/details/buddhisminpracti00lope |format = PDF |url-access = registration }}
* {{Citation| last =Lopez | first =Donald S.| year =2001 | title =The Story of Buddhism | publisher =HarperCollins }}
* {{Citation | last =Lopez | first =Donald, jr. | year =2009 | title =Buddhism and Science: A Guide for the Perplexed | publisher =University of Chicago Press}}
* {{Citation | last=Lopez | first =Donald S. | year =2012 | title =The Scientific Buddha: His Short and Happy Life | publisher =Yale University Press | isbn =978-0-300-15913-4}}
<!-- M -->
* {{Citation | last =Makransky | first =John J. | year =1997 | title =Buddhahood Embodied: Sources of Controversy in India and Tibet | publisher =SUNY}}
* {{Citation | last =McDermott| first=James Paul | year= 1975 |title=The Kathāvatthu Kamma Debates|work=Journal of the American Oriental Society|volume=95 |issue=3}}
* {{Citation | last =McMahan | first =David L. | year =2008 | title =The Making of Buddhist Modernism | publisher =Oxford University Press | isbn =9780195183276}}
* {{Citation| last =Moffitt | first =Philip| year =2008 | title =Dancing with Life: Buddhist Insights for Finding Meaning and Joy in the Face of Suffering |edition= Kindle | publisher =Rodale}}
* {{Citation| last =Monier-Williams | title =A Sanskrit-English Dictionary | place =London | publisher =Oxford University Press | url =http://www.sanskrit-lexicon.uni-koeln.de/scans/MWScan/MWScanpdf/mw0483-dut.pdf | access-date =27 December 2008}}
<!-- N -->
* {{Citation | last =Norman | first =K.R. | year =2003 | chapter =The Four Noble Truths | title =K.R. Norman Collected Papers II | chapter-url =http://www.ahandfulofleaves.org/documents/Articles/The%20Four%20Noble%20Truths_Norman_PTS_2003.pdf}}
* {{Citation | last =Nyanatiloka | year =1980 | title =Buddhist Dictionary | publisher =Buddhist Publication Society}}
<!-- P -->
* {{Citation| last =Potter | first =Karl | year =2004 | title =The Encyclopedia of Indian Philosophies, Vol. IX: Buddhist philosophy from 350 to 600 AD}}
* {{Citation | last =Prothero | first =Stephen R. | year =1996 | title =The White Buddhist: the Asian odyssey of Henry Steel Olcott | publisher =Indiana University Press}}
<!-- R -->
* {{Citation | last =Raju | first =P. T. | year =1985 | title =Structural Depths of Indian Thought | publisher =State University of New York Press | isbn =978-0-88706-139-4 |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=wZ_iahRQomwC}}
* {{Citation |last = Rockhill |first = William Woodville (translator) |author-link = William Woodville Rockhill |year= 1884 |title = The life of the Buddha and the early history of his order, derived from Tibetan works in the Bkah-Hgyur and Bstan-Hgyur, followed by notices on the early history of Tibet and Khoten |location = London | publisher = Trübner |url = https://archive.org/details/lifeofbuddhaearl00rock}}
<!-- S -->
* {{Citation | last =Samuel | first =Geoffrey | year =2008 | title =The Origins of Yoga and Tantra: Indic Religions to the Thirteenth Century | publisher =Cambridge University Press}}
* {{Citation | last =Schmidt-Leukel | first =Perry | year =2006 | title =Understanding Buddhism | publisher =Dunedin Academic Press | isbn =978-1-903765-18-0 | url =https://books.google.com/books?id=3DrYAAAAMAAJ}}
* {{Citation | last =Schmithausen | first =Lambert | year =1981 | title =On some Aspects of Descriptions or Theories of 'Liberating Insight' and 'Enlightenment' in Early Buddhism". In: Studien zum Jainismus und Buddhismus (Gedenkschrift für Ludwig Alsdorf), hrsg. von Klaus Bruhn und Albrecht Wezler, Wiesbaden}}
* {{Citation | last =Schmithausen | first =Lambert | year =1986 | chapter =Critical response | editor-last =Neufeldt | editor-first =Ronald W. | title =Karma and rebirth: Post-Classical Developments | publisher =State University of New York}}
* {{Citation | last =Sharf | first =Robert H. | year =1995 | title =Buddhist Modernism and the Rhetoric of Meditative Experience | journal =NUMEN | volume =42 | issue =3 | pages =228–283 | doi =10.1163/1568527952598549 | hdl =2027.42/43810 | url =http://buddhiststudies.berkeley.edu/people/faculty/sharf/documents/Sharf1995,%20Buddhist%20Modernism.pdf | access-date =4 December 2014 | archive-url =https://web.archive.org/web/20190412103407/http://buddhiststudies.berkeley.edu/people/faculty/sharf/documents/Sharf1995,%20Buddhist%20Modernism.pdf | archive-date =12 April 2019 | url-status =dead | hdl-access =free }}
* {{Citation | last =Sharf | first =Robert H. | year =2000 | title =The Rhetoric of Experience and the Study of Religion | journal =Journal of Consciousness Studies | volume =7 | issue =11–12 | pages =267–87 | url =http://buddhiststudies.berkeley.edu/people/faculty/sharf/documents/Sharf1998,%20Religious%20Experience.pdf | access-date =4 December 2014 | archive-url =https://web.archive.org/web/20130513104227/http://buddhiststudies.berkeley.edu/people/faculty/sharf/documents/Sharf1998,%20Religious%20Experience.pdf | archive-date =13 May 2013 | url-status =dead }}
* {{Citation| last1 =Smith| first1=Huston|last2=Novak|first2=Philip| year=2009| title= Buddhism: A Concise Introduction |edition= Kindle | publisher=HarperOne}}
* {{Citation | last =Snelling | first=John | year= 1987 |title =The Buddhist handbook. A Complete Guide to Buddhist Teaching and Practice | place =London | publisher =Century Paperbacks}}
* {{Citation | last =Spiro | first =Melford E. | year =1982 | title =Buddhism and Society: A Great Tradition and Its Burmese Vicissitudes | publisher =University of California Press}}
<!-- T -->
* {{Citation | last =Taylor | first =Philip | year =2007 | title =Modernity and Re-enchantment: Religion in Post-revolutionary Vietnam | publisher =Institute of Southeast Asian Studies}}
* {{Citation | last =Trainor | first =Kevin | year =2004 | title =Buddhism: The Illustrated Guide | publisher =Oxford University Press | isbn =978-0-19-517398-7 | url=https://books.google.com/books?id=_PrloTKuAjwC}}
<!-- V -->
* {{Citation | last =Vetter | first =Tilmann | year =1988 | title =The Ideas and Meditative Practices of Early Buddhism | publisher =BRILL}}
<!-- W -->
* {{Citation |last=Wallace |first =B. Alan | year =2002 |chapter =The Spectrum of Buddhist Practice in the West |editor-last1 =Prebish |editor-first1= Charles S. |editor-last2= Baumann |editor-first2 =Martin |title= Westward Dharma: Buddhism Beyond Asia |publisher= University of California Press |isbn= 0-520-23490-1 |url= https://books.google.com/books?id=IU4-xZ2c2PIC}}
* {{Citation |last=Warder |first=A.K. |year =1999 |title=Indian Buddhism |place =Delhi}}
* {{Citation |last=Watson |first= Burton | year=1993 |title = The Lotus Sutra |publisher= Columbia University Press}}
* {{Citation |last=Williams |first=Paul |year=1989 |title= Mahayana Buddhism}}
* {{Citation| last= Williams |first=Paul | year =2008 | title =Mahayana Buddhism: The Doctrinal Foundations | publisher = Routledge}}
* {{Citation |last1=Williams |first1=Paul |last2=Tribe |first2=Anthony |last3=Wynne |first3=Alexander |year=2002 |title=Buddhist Thought |edition=Kindle |publisher=Taylor & Francis}}
* {{Citation |last1=Williams |first1=Paul |last2=Tribe |first2=Anthony |last3=Wynne |first3=Alexander |year=2012 |title=Buddhist Thought |edition=2nd |publisher=Routledge |isbn=978-1-136-52088-4 |url= https://books.google.com/books?id=NOLfCgAAQBAJ}}
{{div col end}}
{{Refend}}

=== References ===
[[Category:Pages]]
